generatedSentence: In a world where digital mayhem could vilify even the royal, a group of mindful rockers in jumpsuits kept their backstage refuge hidden in midtown. Their founder, a former trooper with pixel-sharp eyes and a transistor keyboard, often shared balmy daydreams of enlightenment, teaching his group the knowhow to navigate the offline threats lurking in the urban biome; he'd remark, often joking, that true light was an obvious antidote to the malefic gremlin of bigotry, a constant curse they sought to muffle with their musical art. One weeks-long inquiry revealed a lowdown plan to hijack the city's ozone generators, a globalist plot for injustice orchestrated by a kabuki-masked fanatic and his accomplice, a watcher who’d vitrify dissent with a weapon designed to pull the very gravity from the air, causing hiccups in all tech systems and turning ordinary citizens into a grouper of layaway-obsessed killjoys via videos broadcast on a parliamentary pole. This wicked idea demanded a jailbreak from the shadows; a volunteer firefighter with a thorium-powered revolver and a necktie of oranges – a symbolic trooper of the country’s resilience – would push through the backdoor of the foundry, past the alphabetic entries of the cabinet, to occupy the fulcrum of this deformation, knowing the great mother university was educating veejays to rejoice in freedom by switched code, making the effort effortless, a grand tantrum against the dragon of tyranny, to busted the rivet of evil and import a new era of peace, and no longer suffer its affrays, achieving self-endurance for oneself and all under a digital oak.
panelSentences: In a world where digital mayhem could vilify even the royal, a group of mindful rockers in jumpsuits kept their backstage refuge hidden in midtown.

Their founder, a former trooper with pixel-sharp eyes and a transistor keyboard, often shared balmy daydreams of enlightenment, teaching his group the knowhow to navigate the offline threats lurking in the urban biome; he'd remark, often joking, that true light was an obvious antidote to the malefic gremlin of bigotry, a constant curse they sought to muffle with their musical art.

One weeks-long inquiry revealed a lowdown plan to hijack the city's ozone generators, a globalist plot for injustice orchestrated by a kabuki-masked fanatic and his accomplice, a watcher who’d vitrify dissent with a weapon designed to pull the very gravity from the air, causing hiccups in all tech systems and turning ordinary citizens into a grouper of layaway-obsessed killjoys via videos broadcast on a parliamentary pole.

This wicked idea demanded a jailbreak from the shadows; a volunteer firefighter with a thorium-powered revolver and a necktie of oranges – a symbolic trooper of the country’s resilience – would push through the backdoor of the foundry, past the alphabetic entries of the cabinet, to occupy the fulcrum of this deformation, knowing the great mother university was educating veejays to rejoice in freedom by switched code, making the effort effortless, a grand tantrum against the dragon of tyranny, to busted the rivet of evil and import a new era of peace, and no longer suffer its affrays, achieving self-endurance for oneself and all under a digital oak.
rawPanelSentences: In a **world** where digital **mayhem** could **vilify** even the **royal**, a **group** of **mindful** **rockers** in **jumpsuits** kept their **backstage** **refuge** hidden in **midtown**.

Their **founder**, a former **trooper** with **pixel**-sharp **eyes** and a **transistor** **keyboard**, often shared **balmy** **daydreams** of **enlightenment**, teaching his **group** the **knowhow** to navigate the **offline** threats lurking in the urban **biome**; he'd **remark**, often **joking**, that true **light** was an **obvious** antidote to the **malefic** **gremlin** of **bigotry**, a constant **curse** they sought to **muffle** with their **musical** art.

One **weeks**-long **inquiry** revealed a **lowdown** plan to **hijack** the city's **ozone** generators, a **globalist** plot for **injustice** orchestrated by a **kabuki**-masked **fanatic** and his **accomplice**, a **watcher** who’d **vitrify** dissent with a **weapon** designed to **pull** the very **gravity** from the air, causing **hiccups** in all tech systems and turning ordinary citizens into a **grouper** of **layaway**-obsessed **killjoy**s via **videos** broadcast on a **parliamentary** **pole**.

This **wicked** **idea** demanded a **jailbreak** from the shadows; a **volunteer** **firefighter** with a **thorium**-powered **revolver** and a **necktie** of **oranges** – a symbolic **trooper** of the **country**’s resilience – would **push** through the **backdoor** of the **foundry**, past the **alphabetic** **entries** of the **cabinet**, to **occupy** the **fulcrum** of this **deformation**, knowing the **great** **mother** **university** was **educating** **veejays** to **rejoice** in freedom by **switched** code, making the effort **effortless**, a grand **tantrum** against the **dragon** of tyranny, to **busted** the **rivet** of evil and **import** a new era of peace, and no longer **suffer** its **affrays**, achieving self-**endurance** for **oneself** and all under a digital **oak**.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. A cyber-royal in a luminous thorium-powered jumpsuit, carrying a custom-built revolver with pixelated grips. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A diverse group of rockers in glowing jumpsuits playing futuristic instruments on a grimy midtown rooftop, city lights twinkling below, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The cyber-royal protagonist, eyes fixed on a holographic map showing a shadowy figure (the fanatic) near a large ozone generator, medium shot, tense expression. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, now a firefighter, in a dramatic pose, firing the thorium-powered revolver at a menacing, dragon-like drone within a glowing foundry, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist standing triumphantly beside a digital oak tree in a peaceful, regenerated urban biome, holographic screens displaying 'freedom' in various fonts, serene wide shot.

definitions.definition: To say defamatory things about someone or something; to speak ill of.
To belittle through speech; to put down.
To convert into, or cause to resemble, glass or a glassy substance, by heat and fusion.
To be converted into glass, especially through heat.
An instrument of attack or defense in combat or hunting, e.g. most guns, missiles, or swords.
definitions.example: The club that is now mostly used for golf was once a common weapon.

definitions.definition: An instrument or other means of harming or exerting control over another.
definitions.example: Money is the main weapon of modern oligarchs.

definitions.definition: A tool of any kind.
definitions.example: Choose your weapon.

definitions.definition: An idiot, an oaf, a fool, a tool; a contemptible or incompetent person.
A stage performance, show or film that involves singing, dancing and musical numbers performed by the cast as well as acting.
A meeting or a party for a musical entertainment; a musicale.
Of, belonging or relating to music, or to its performance or notation.
definitions.example: musical proportion; musical instruments

definitions.definition: Pleasing to the ear; sounding agreeably; having the qualities of music; melodious; harmonious.
definitions.example: She had a musical voice.

definitions.definition: Fond of music; discriminating with regard to music; gifted or skilled in music.
definitions.example: the child is musical; having a musical ear

definitions.definition: Pertaining to a class of games in which players move while music plays, but have to take a fixed position when it stops; by extension, any situation where people repeatedly change positions.
A malefic planet
Having an evil or harmful influence; baleful.
Any major regional biological community such as that of forest or desert
All the genomes of such a community
A person's self: general form of himself, herself, themself or yourself.
definitions.example: Teaching oneself to swim can be dangerous.

definitions.definition: An allotrope of oxygen (symbol O₃) having three atoms in the molecule instead of the usual two; it is a blue gas, generated from oxygen by electrical discharge.
Fresh air, especially that breathed at the seaside and smelling of seaweed.
To treat with ozone.
A royal person; a member of a royal family.
A standard size of printing paper, measuring 25 by 20 inches.
A standard size of writing paper, measuring 24 by 19 inches.
The Australian decimal currency intended to replace the pound in 1966; was changed to "dollar" before it was actually circulated.
Any of various lycaenid butterflies.
The fourth tine of an antler's beam.
A stag with twelve points (six on each antler).
In large sailing ships, square sail over the topgallant sail.
An old English gold coin, the rial.
A small mortar.
In auction bridge, a royal spade.
A tuft of beard on the lower lip.
(campanology) Bell changes rung on ten bells.
Of or relating to a monarch or their family.
Having the air or demeanour of a monarch; illustrious; magnanimous; of more than common size or excellence.
In large sailing ships, of a mast right above the topgallant mast and its sails.
definitions.example: royal mast;  royal sail

definitions.definition: Free-for-all, especially involving multiple combatants.
Used as an intensifier.
definitions.example: a royal pain in the neck

definitions.definition: A state of safety, protection or shelter.
A place providing safety, protection or shelter.
Something or someone turned to for safety or assistance; a recourse or resort.
An expedient to secure protection or defence.
A refuge island.
To return to a place of shelter.
To shelter; to protect.
A mythical creature reputed to be mischievously inclined to damage or dismantle machinery.
(by extension) Any mysterious, unknown source of trouble or mischief.
definitions.example: We rechecked everything, and we suspect gremlins in the database.

definitions.definition: A young inexperienced surfer.
A supernatural detriment or hindrance; a bane.
A prayer or imprecation that harm may befall someone.
The cause of great harm, evil, or misfortune; that which brings evil or severe affliction; torment.
A vulgar epithet.
(usually with "the") A woman's menses.
A state or situation of great confusion, disorder, trouble or destruction; chaos.
definitions.example: In all the mayhem, some children were separated from their partners.

definitions.definition: Infliction of violent injury on a person or thing.
definitions.example: The fighting dogs created mayhem in the flower beds.

definitions.definition: The maiming of a person by depriving him of the use of any of his limbs which are necessary for defense or protection.
The crime of damaging things or harming people on purpose.
The part of a city between uptown and downtown

Visible electromagnetic radiation. The human eye can typically detect radiation (light) in the wavelength range of about 400 to 750 nanometers. Nearby shorter and longer wavelength ranges, although not visible, are commonly called ultraviolet and infrared light.
definitions.example: As you can see, this spacious dining-room gets a lot of light in the mornings.

definitions.definition: A source of illumination.
definitions.example: Put that light out!

definitions.definition: Spiritual or mental illumination; enlightenment, useful information.
definitions.example: Can you throw any light on this problem?

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Facts; pieces of information; ideas, concepts.
A notable person within a specific field or discipline.
definitions.example: Picasso was one of the leading lights of the cubist movement.

definitions.definition: The manner in which the light strikes a picture; that part of a picture which represents those objects upon which the light is supposed to fall; the more illuminated part of a landscape or other scene; opposed to shade.
A point of view, or aspect from which a concept, person or thing is regarded.
definitions.example: I'm really seeing you in a different light today.

definitions.definition: A flame or something used to create fire.
definitions.example: Hey, buddy, you got a light?

definitions.definition: A firework made by filling a case with a substance which burns brilliantly with a white or coloured flame.
definitions.example: a Bengal light

definitions.definition: A window, or space for a window in architecture.
definitions.example: This facade has eight south-facing lights.

definitions.definition: The series of squares reserved for the answer to a crossword clue.
definitions.example: The average length of a light on a 15×15 grid is 7 or 8.

definitions.definition: A cross-light in a double acrostic or triple acrostic.
Open view; a visible state or condition; public observation; publicity.
The power of perception by vision.
The brightness of the eye or eyes.
A traffic light, or, by extension, an intersection controlled by one or more that will face a traveler who is receiving instructions.
definitions.example: To get to our house, turn right at the third light.

definitions.definition: To take (a system, etc.) offline; to demote from an active or online state.

Of a system, currently not connected (generally electrically) to a larger network. For example, a power plant which is not connected to the grid, or a computer which is not connected to the Internet or to any other communications service.
definitions.example: I saved the web pages so I could read them later offline.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Happening in the physical world (the real world) as opposed to on the internet.
definitions.example: offline dating

definitions.definition: While offline; with an offline system.
Outside the current meeting, in a more private setting.
(of time) To take or use.
To take or use space.
To have sexual intercourse with.
To do business in; to busy oneself with.
To use; to expend; to make use of.
A spasm of the diaphragm, or the resulting sound.
definitions.example: There was a loud hiccup from the back of the room and the class erupted in laughter.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Any spasm or sudden change.
A minor setback.
definitions.example: There's been a slight hiccup in the processing of this quarter's results.

definitions.definition: To produce a hiccup; have the hiccups.
To say with a hiccup.
definitions.example: "I haven't touched a drop, officer," the suspect hiccupped.

definitions.definition: To produce an abortive sound like a hiccup.
definitions.example: The car engine hiccupped but wouldn't start.

definitions.definition: (usually with "the") The condition of having the spasms of hiccupping.
definitions.example: He has the hiccups - give him a glass of water.

definitions.definition: An act of pointing out or noticing; notice or observation.
An expression, in speech or writing, of something remarked or noticed; a mention of something
definitions.example: a biting remark

definitions.definition: A casual observation, comment, or statement
To make a remark or remarks; to comment.
To express in words or writing; to state; to make a comment
definitions.example: He remarked that it was getting late.

definitions.definition: To pay heed to; notice; to take notice of
To mark in a notable manner; to distinguish clearly; to make noticeable or conspicuous; to point out.
(engraving) A small design etched on the margin of a plate and supposed to be removed after the earliest proofs have been taken.
(engraving) Any feature distinguishing a particular stage of the plate.
(engraving) A print or proof distinguished by such a mark.
A handgun with a revolving chamber enabling several shots to be fired without reloading.
(by extension) Any (personal) firearm with such a mechanism.
Synonym of revolving line of credit
Agent noun of revolve; something that revolves.
A person who is anti-fun, or prevents others from having fun.
A number of things or persons being in some relation to one another.
definitions.example: A group of people gathered in front of the Parliament to demonstrate against the Prime Minister's proposals.

definitions.definition: A set with an associative binary operation, under which there exists an identity element, and such that each element has an inverse.
An effective divisor on a curve.
A (usually small) group of people who perform music together.
definitions.example: Did you see the new jazz group?

definitions.definition: A small number (up to about fifty) of galaxies that are near each other.
A column in the periodic table of chemical elements.
A functional group.
definitions.example: Nitro is an electron-withdrawing group.

definitions.definition: A subset of a culture or of a society.
An air force formation.
A collection of formations or rock strata.
A number of users with same rights with respect to accession, modification, and execution of files, computers and peripherals.
An element of an espresso machine from which hot water pours into the portafilter.
A number of eighth, sixteenth, etc., notes joined at the stems; sometimes rather indefinitely applied to any ornament made up of a few short notes.
A set of teams playing each other in the same division, while not during the same period playing any teams that belong to other sets in the division.
A commercial organization.
To put together to form a group.
definitions.example: group the dogs by hair colour

definitions.definition: To come together to form a group.
A storage closet either separate from, or built into, a wall.
A cupboard.
The upright assembly that houses a coin-operated arcade game, a cab.
A size of photograph, specifically one measuring 3⅞" by 5½".
A group of advisors to a government or business entity.

(often capitalized) In parliamentary and some other systems of government, the group of ministers responsible for creating government policy and for overseeing the departments comprising the executive branch.
A small chamber or private room.
(often capitalized) A collection of art or ethnographic objects.
(Rhode Island) Milkshake.
A hut; a cottage; a small house.
An enclosure for mechanical or electrical equipment.
Being aware (of something); attentive, heedful.
Inclined (to do something).
To do or say something for amusement rather than seriously.
definitions.example: I didn’t mean what I said — I was only joking.

definitions.definition: (intransitive, followed by with) To dupe in a friendly manner for amusement; to mess with, play with.
definitions.example: Relax, man, I'm just joking with you.

definitions.definition: To make merry with; to make jokes upon; to rally.
definitions.example: to joke a comrade

definitions.definition: The act of telling or engaging in jokes.
A parliamentary train.
Of, relating to, or enacted by a parliament
definitions.example: Parliamentary procedures are sometimes slow.

definitions.definition: Having the supreme executive and legislative power resting with a cabinet of ministers chosen from, and responsible to a parliament.
definitions.example: Britain is a parliamentary democracy.

definitions.definition: Of a class of train which, by an act of parliament, ran both ways along a line, at least once each day, at the rate of one penny per mile.
One of the tiny dots that make up the representation of an image in a computer's memory.
One of the squares that make up a pixel art work or a zoomed in image in a computer.
To create computer graphics by editing individual pixels.
Easily discovered, seen, or understood; self-explanatory.
A spontaneous and fanciful series of thoughts while awake not connected to immediate reality.
Any period of seven consecutive days.
A period of seven days beginning with Sunday or Monday.
A period of five days beginning with Monday.
A subdivision of the month into longer periods of work days punctuated by shorter weekend periods of days for markets, rest, or religious observation such as a sabbath.
definitions.example: A 4-day week consists of Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday.

definitions.definition: Seven days after (sometimes before) a specified date.
definitions.example: I'll see you Thursday week.

definitions.definition: Characteristic qualities of a bigot: (especially religious or racial) intolerant prejudice, opinionatedness, or fanaticism; fanatic intolerance.
Obstinate prejudice or opinionatedness
A one-piece item of clothing originally used by parachutists
A similar item of clothing used for outdoor sports such as skiing
Originally, a stick; now specifically, a long and slender piece of metal or (especially) wood, used for various construction or support purposes.
A type of basic fishing rod.
A long sports implement used for pole-vaulting; now made of glassfiber or carbon fiber, formerly also metal, bamboo and wood have been used.
(spotting) A telescope used to identify birds, aeroplanes or wildlife.
A unit of length, equal to a rod (1/4 chain or 5 1/2 yards).
Pole position.
A gun.
A penis
To propel by pushing with poles, to push with a pole.
definitions.example: Huck Finn poled that raft southward down the Mississippi because going northward against the current was too much work.

definitions.definition: To identify something quite precisely using a telescope.
definitions.example: He poled off the serial of the Gulfstream to confirm its identity.

definitions.definition: To furnish with poles for support.
definitions.example: to pole beans or hops

definitions.definition: To convey on poles.
definitions.example: to pole hay into a barn

definitions.definition: To stir, as molten glass, with a pole.
To strike (the ball) very hard.
A curved piece of wood attached to the bottom of a rocking chair or cradle that enables it to rock back and forth.
A rocking chair.
The lengthwise curvature of a surfboard. (More rocker is a more curved board.)
definitions.example: All modern surfboards share a similar rocker design — Bruce Jones https//web.archive.org/web/20051212041317/http://www.brucejones.com/longboar.htm

definitions.definition: The breve below as in ḫ.
Someone passionate about rock music.
A musician who plays rock music.
A rock music song.
One who rocks something.
A member of a British subculture of the 1960s, opposed to the mods, who dressed in black leather and were interested in 1950s music.
Any implement or machine working with a rocking motion, such as a trough mounted on rockers for separating gold dust from gravel, etc., by agitation in water.
A tool with small teeth that roughens a metal plate to produce tonality in mezzotints.
A rocking horse.
A rocker board.
A skate with a curved blade, somewhat resembling in shape the rocker of a cradle.
A rock shaft.
(with "the") Human collective existence; existence in general.
definitions.example: There will always be lovers, till the world’s end.

definitions.definition: The Universe.
(with "the") The Earth.
definitions.example: People are dying of starvation all over the world.

definitions.definition: A planet, especially one which is inhabited or inhabitable.
definitions.example: Our mission is to travel the galaxy and find new worlds.

definitions.definition: A very large extent of country.
(speculation) A realm, such as planet, containing one or multiple societies of beings, especially intelligent ones.
definitions.example: the world of Narnia; the Wizarding World of Harry Potter; a zombie world

definitions.definition: An individual or group perspective or social setting.
definitions.example: In the world of boxing, good diet is all-important.

definitions.definition: The part of an operating system distributed with the kernel, consisting of the shell and other programs.
A subdivision of a game, consisting of a series of stages or levels that usually share a similar environment or theme.
definitions.example: Have you reached the boss at the end of the ice world?

definitions.definition: The twenty-second trump or major arcana card of the tarot.
A great amount.
definitions.example: Taking a break from work seems to have done her a world of good.

definitions.definition: Age, era
To consider or cause to be considered from a global perspective; to consider as a global whole, rather than making or focussing on national or other distinctions; compare globalise.
To make real; to make worldly.
People who are wicked.
Evil or mischievous by nature.
Excellent; awesome; masterful.
definitions.example: That was a wicked guitar solo, bro!

definitions.definition: Very, extremely.
definitions.example: The band we went to see the other night was wicked loud!

definitions.definition: A person of major significance, accomplishment or acclaim.
definitions.example: Newton and Einstein are two of the greats of the history of science.

definitions.definition: The main division in a pipe organ, usually the loudest division.
(in combinations such as "two-greats", "three-greats" etc.) An instance of the word "great" signifying an additional generation in phrases expressing family relationships.
definitions.example: My three-greats grandmother.

definitions.definition: Relatively large in scale, size, extent, number (i.e. having many parts or members) or duration (i.e. relatively long); very big.
definitions.example: A great storm is approaching our shores.

definitions.definition: Of larger size or more importance than others of its kind.
definitions.example: the great auk

definitions.definition: (qualifying nouns of family relationship) Involving more generations than the qualified word implies — as many extra generations as repetitions of the word great (from 1510s). [see Derived terms]
definitions.example: great-grandfather, great-great-grandfather, great-great-great-grandfather

definitions.definition: (followed by 'with') Pregnant; large with young; full of.
definitions.example: great with child

definitions.definition: (except with 'friend' and similar words such as 'mate','buddy') Intimate; familiar.
Extreme or more than usual.
definitions.example: great worry

definitions.definition: Of significant importance or consequence; important.
definitions.example: a great decision

definitions.definition: (applied to actions, thoughts and feelings) Arising from or possessing idealism; admirable; superior; commanding; heroic; illustrious; eminent.
definitions.example: a great deed

definitions.definition: Impressive or striking.
definitions.example: a great show of wealth

definitions.definition: Much in use; favoured.
definitions.example: Poetry was a great convention of the Romantic era.

definitions.definition: (applied to persons) Endowed with extraordinary powers; of exceptional talents or achievements; uncommonly gifted; able to accomplish vast results; remarkable; strong; powerful; mighty; noble.
definitions.example: a great hero, scholar, genius, philosopher, writer etc.

definitions.definition: Title referring to an important leader.
definitions.example: Alexander the Great

definitions.definition: Doing or exemplifying (a characteristic or pursuit) on a large scale; active or enthusiastic.
definitions.example: He's not a great one for reading.

definitions.definition: (often followed by 'at') Skilful or adroit.
definitions.example: You are great at singing.

definitions.definition: Very good; excellent; wonderful; fantastic (from 1848).
definitions.example: Dinner was great.

definitions.definition: Intensifying a word or expression, used in mild oaths.
definitions.example: Great Scott!

definitions.definition: Very well (in a very satisfactory manner).
definitions.example: Those mechanical colored pencils work great because they don't have to be sharpened.

definitions.definition: Expression of gladness and content about something.
definitions.example: Great! Thanks for the wonderful work.

definitions.definition: Sarcastic inversion thereof.
definitions.example: Oh, great! I just dumped all 500 sheets of the manuscript all over and now I have to put them back in order.

definitions.definition: A (human) female who has given birth to a baby
definitions.example: He had something of his mother in him.

definitions.definition: A human female who parents an adopted or fostered child
A human female who donates a fertilized egg or donates a body cell which has resulted in a clone.
A pregnant female, possibly as a shortened form of mother-to-be.
definitions.example: Nutrients and oxygen obtained by the mother are conveyed to the fetus.

definitions.definition: A female parent of an animal.
definitions.example: The lioness was a mother of four cubs.

definitions.definition: A female ancestor.
A source or origin.
definitions.example: The Mediterranean was mother to many cultures and languages.

definitions.definition: Something that is the greatest or most significant of its kind. (See mother of all.)
(when followed by a surname) A title of respect for one's mother-in-law.
definitions.example: Mother Smith, meet my cousin, Doug Jones.

definitions.definition: Any elderly woman, especially within a particular community.
Any person or entity which performs mothering.
The principal piece of an astrolabe, into which the others are fixed.
The female superior or head of a religious house; an abbess, etc.
Hysterical passion; hysteria; the uterus.
The area behind a stage out of view of the audience where performers wait to give their show; especially that of the dressing rooms.
The private portion of the space used by a business or enterprise.
Relating to, or situated in the area behind a stage.
Secret, concealed from the public.
Towards the area behind the stage.
Out of view of the public.
A soldier of private rank in cavalry or armour.
A cavalry horse; charger.
A soldier.
A troopship.
A state trooper.
A mounted policeman.
One who endures adversity or hardship with an attitude of stoicism and persistence.
definitions.example: He was a real trooper about taking care of the kids for the weekend.

definitions.definition: To work as a trooper.
To work steadily at an unpleasant job without complaint.
(semiconductors) A solid-state semiconductor device, with three terminals, which can be used for amplification, switching, voltage stabilization, signal modulation, and many other functions.
A transistor radio.
An escape from prison.
To escape from prison.
To modify the firmware of an electronic device, especially a mobile phone, in order to remove restrictions that prevent it from running unofficial software.
One who founds or establishes (especially said of a company, project, organisation, state)
definitions.example: the founder of Facebook, Mark Zuckerberg

definitions.definition: Someone for whose parents one has no data.
A deciduous tree with distinctive deeply lobed leaves, acorns, and notably strong wood, typically of England and northeastern North America, included in genus Quercus.
The wood of the oak.
A rich brown colour, like that of oak wood.
Any tree of the genus Quercus, in family Fagaceae.
Any tree of other genera and species of trees resembling typical oaks of genus Quercus in some ways.
The outer (lockable) door of a set of rooms in a college or similar institution. (Often in the phrase "to sport one's oak").
The flavor of oak.
To expose to oak in order for the oak to impart its flavors.
Having a rich brown colour, like that of oak wood.
Made of oak wood or timber
definitions.example: an oak table, oak beam, etc

definitions.definition: An organ through which animals see (perceive surroundings via light).
definitions.example: Bright lights really hurt my eyes.

definitions.definition: The visual sense.
definitions.example: The car was quite pleasing to the eye, but impractical.

definitions.definition: The iris of the eye, being of a specified colour.
definitions.example: Brown, blue, green, hazel eyes.

definitions.definition: Attention, notice.
definitions.example: That dress caught her eye.

definitions.definition: The ability to notice what others might miss.
definitions.example: He has an eye for talent.

definitions.definition: A meaningful stare or look.
definitions.example: She was giving him the eye at the bar.

definitions.definition: A private eye: a privately hired detective or investigator.
A hole at the blunt end of a needle through which thread is passed.
The oval hole of an axehead through which the axehandle is fitted.
A fitting consisting of a loop of metal or other material, suitable for receiving a hook or the passage of a cord or line.
The relatively clear and calm center of a hurricane or other cyclonic storm.
A mark on an animal, such as a peacock or butterfly, resembling a human eye.
The dark spot on a black-eyed pea.
A reproductive bud in a potato.
The dark brown center of a black-eyed Susan flower.

A loop forming part of anything, or a hole through anything, to receive a rope, hook, pin, shaft, etc. — e.g. at the end of a tie bar in a bridge truss; through a crank; at the end of a rope; or through a millstone.
That which resembles the eye in relative importance or beauty.
Tinge; shade of colour.
One of the holes in certain kinds of cheese.
The circle in the centre of a volute.
The enclosed counter (negative space) of the small letter e.
(game of go) An empty point or group of points surrounded by one player's stones.
(usually plural) View or opinion.

definitions.definition: To observe carefully or appraisingly.
definitions.example: After eyeing the document for half an hour, she decided not to sign it.

definitions.definition: To appear; to look.
A brood.
definitions.example: an eye of pheasants

definitions.definition: An act of enlightening, or the state of being enlightened or instructed.
A concept in spirituality, philosophy and psychology related to achieving clarity of perception, reason and knowledge.
The short term state of the atmosphere at a specific time and place, including the temperature, relative humidity, cloud cover, precipitation, wind, etc.
Unpleasant or destructive atmospheric conditions, and their effects.
definitions.example: Wooden garden furniture must be well oiled as it is continuously exposed to weather.

definitions.definition: The direction from which the wind is blowing; used attributively to indicate the windward side.
A situation.
A storm; a tempest.
A light shower of rain.
To expose to the weather, or show the effects of such exposure, or to withstand such effects.
(by extension) To sustain the trying effect of; to bear up against and overcome; to endure; to resist.
To break down, of rocks and other materials, under the effects of exposure to rain, sunlight, temperature, and air.
To pass to windward in a vessel, especially to beat 'round.
definitions.example: to weather a cape; to weather another ship

definitions.definition: To endure or survive an event or action without undue damage.
definitions.example: Joshua weathered a collision with a freighter near South Africa.

definitions.definition: To place (a hawk) unhooded in the open air.
Facing towards the flow of a fluid, usually air.
A subsidiary entrance to a building or house at its rear, normally away from the street.
A means of access, often secret and unprotected, to something.
A secret means of access to a program or system.
A rear side door of a car, or at the back of a van.
The anus, generally used in reference to anal sex.
The rear side of the hole, furthest from the golfer.
To attempt to accomplish by indirect means, especially when direct means are proscribed.
To enter a tube by accelerating from behind; to surf into an already formed hollow wave, in contrast to the normal method of slowing to allow a surfable wave to form.
A legendary serpentine or reptilian creature.
An animal of various species that resemble a dragon in appearance:
(with definite article, often capitalized) The constellation Draco.
A fierce and unpleasant woman; a harridan.
definitions.example: She’s a bit of a dragon.

definitions.definition: (with definite article, often capitalized) The (historical) Chinese empire or the People's Republic of China.
definitions.example: Napoleon already warned of the awakening of the Dragon.

definitions.definition: Something very formidable or dangerous.
A type of playing-tile (red dragon, green dragon, white dragon) in the game of mahjong.
A luminous exhalation from marshy ground, seeming to move through the air like a winged serpent.
A short musket hooked to a swivel attached to a soldier's belt; so called from a representation of a dragon's head at the muzzle.
A background process similar to a daemon.
A variety of carrier pigeon.
Television, television show, movie.
A short film clip, with or without audio (as in a music video, or one of the plethora of user-generated short movies on sites such as YouTube).
Motion picture stored on VHS or some other format.
VHS.
Producing balm.
Soothing or fragrant.
Mild and pleasant.
Foolish; slightly crazy or mad; eccentric.
Strange, similar to the effects of a hallucinogen.
An act of pulling (applying force)
definitions.example: He gave the hair a sharp pull and it came out.

definitions.definition: An attractive force which causes motion towards the source
definitions.example: She took a pull on her cigarette.

definitions.definition: Any device meant to be pulled, as a lever, knob, handle, or rope
definitions.example: a zipper pull

definitions.definition: Something in one's favour in a comparison or a contest; an advantage; means of influencing.
definitions.example: In weights the favourite had the pull.

definitions.definition: Appeal or attraction (e.g. of a movie star)
The situation where a client sends out a request for data from a server, as in server pull, pull technology
A journey made by rowing
A contest; a struggle.
definitions.example: va wrestling pull}}

definitions.definition: Loss or violence suffered.
The act of drinking; a mouthful or swig of a drink.
definitions.example: to take a pull at a mug of beer

definitions.definition: A kind of stroke by which a leg ball is sent to the off side, or an off ball to the side.
A mishit shot which travels in a straight line and (for a right-handed player) left of the intended path.
A single impression from a handpress.
To apply a force to (an object) so that it comes toward the person or thing applying the force.
definitions.example: When I give the signal, pull the rope.

definitions.definition: To gather with the hand, or by drawing toward oneself; to pluck.

definitions.definition: To attract or net; to pull in.
To persuade (someone) to have sex with one.

definitions.definition: To remove (something), especially from public circulation or availability.
definitions.example: Each day, they pulled the old bread and set out fresh loaves.

definitions.definition: To retrieve or generate for use.
definitions.example: I'll have to pull a part number for that.

definitions.definition: To do or perform.
definitions.example: He regularly pulls 12-hour days, sometimes 14.

definitions.definition: (with 'a' and the name of a person, place, event, etc.) To copy or emulate the actions or behaviour that is associated with the person or thing mentioned.

definitions.definition: To toss a frisbee with the intention of launching the disc across the length of a field.
To row.
To achieve by rowing on a rowing machine.
definitions.example: I pulled a personal best on the erg yesterday.

definitions.definition: To draw apart; to tear; to rend.
To strain (a muscle, tendon, ligament, etc.).
To draw (a hostile non-player character) into combat, or toward or away from some location or target.
To score a certain number of points in a sport.
definitions.example: How many points did you pull today, Albert?

definitions.definition: To hold back, and so prevent from winning.
definitions.example: The favourite was pulled.

definitions.definition: To take or make (a proof or impression); so called because hand presses were worked by pulling a lever.
To strike the ball in a particular manner. (See noun sense.)
To draw beer from a pump, keg, or other source.
definitions.example: Let's stop at Finnigan's. The barman pulls a good pint.

definitions.definition: (rail transportation, of a railroad car) To pull out from a yard or station; to leave.
To pluck or pick (flowers, fruit etc.).
Command used by a target shooter to request that the target be released/launched.
Any place of shelter.

definitions.definition: A sheltered expanse of water, adjacent to land, in which ships may anchor or dock, especially for loading and unloading.
(glassworking) A mixing box for materials.
A house of the zodiac, or the mansion of a heavenly body.
Shelter, refuge.
To undergo hardship.

definitions.definition: To feel pain.
definitions.example: At least he didn't suffer when he died in the car crash.

definitions.definition: To become worse.
definitions.example: If you keep partying like this, your school-work will suffer.

definitions.definition: To endure, undergo.

definitions.definition: To allow.
The measure of a person's stamina or persistence.
definitions.example: He has great endurance: he ran a marathon and then cycled home.

definitions.definition: Ability to endure hardship.
The length of time that a ship's rations will supply
To be very happy, be delighted, exult; to feel joy.
To have (someone) as a lover or spouse; to enjoy sexually.
To make happy, exhilarate.
To enjoy.
(etc.) A set of keys used to operate a typewriter, computer etc.
A component of many instruments including the piano, organ, and harpsichord consisting of usually black and white keys that cause different tones to be produced when struck.
A device with keys of a musical keyboard, used to control electronic sound-producing devices which may be built into or separate from the keyboard device.
To type on a computer keyboard.
definitions.example: Keyboarding is the part of this job I hate the most.

definitions.definition: The state or condition of having weight; weight; heaviness.
The state or condition of being grave; seriousness.

definitions.definition: The lowness of a note.
Force on Earth's surface, of the attraction by the Earth's masses, and the centrifugal pseudo-force caused by the Earth's rotation, resulting from gravitation.
(in casual discussion, also) Gravitation, universal force exercised by two bodies onto each other (gravity and gravitation are often used interchangeably).
Specific gravity.
Anything that mutes or deadens sound.
A warm piece of clothing for the hands.
A boxing glove.
A kiln or furnace, often electric, with no direct flames (a muffle furnace)
The bare end of the nose between the nostrils, especially in ruminants.
A machine with two pulleys to hoist load by spinning wheels, polyspast, block and tackle.
To wrap (a person, face etc.) in fabric or another covering, for warmth or protection; often with up.
To wrap up or cover (a source of noise) in order to deaden the sound.

definitions.definition: To mute or deaden (a sound etc.).
To speak indistinctly, or without clear articulation.
To prevent seeing, or hearing, or speaking, by wraps bound about the head; to blindfold; to deafen.
A short, directed application of force; an act of pushing.

definitions.definition: An act of tensing the muscles of the abdomen in order to expel its contents.
definitions.example: One more push and the baby will be out.

definitions.definition: A great effort (to do something).
definitions.example: Let's give one last push on our advertising campaign.

definitions.definition: An attempt to persuade someone into a particular course of action.
A marching or drill maneuver/manoeuvre performed by moving a formation (especially a company front) forward or toward the audience, usually to accompany a dramatic climax or crescendo in the music.
A wager that results in no loss or gain for the bettor as a result of a tie or even score
The addition of a data item to the top of a stack.
The situation where a server sends data to a client without waiting for a request.

definitions.definition: A particular crowd or throng or people.
A foul shot in which the cue ball is in contact with the cue and the object ball at the same time
To apply a force to (an object) such that it moves away from the person or thing applying the force.

definitions.definition: To continually attempt to persuade (a person) into a particular course of action.
To press or urge forward; to drive.
definitions.example: to push an objection too far; to push one's luck

definitions.definition: To continually promote (a point of view, a product for sale, etc.).

definitions.definition: To continually exert oneself in order to achieve a goal.
To approach; to come close to.
definitions.example: He's pushing sixty. (= he's nearly sixty years old)

definitions.definition: To tense the muscles in the abdomen in order to expel its contents.
definitions.example: During childbirth, there are times when the obstetrician advises the woman not to push.

definitions.definition: To continue to attempt to persuade a person into a particular course of action.
To make a higher bid at an auction.
To make an all-in bet.
To move (a pawn) directly forward.
To add (a data item) to the top of a stack.
To publish (an update, etc.) by transmitting it to other computers.
To thrust the points of the horns against; to gore.
To burst out of its pot, as a bud or shoot.
To strike the cue ball in such a way that it stays in contact with the cue and object ball at the same time (a foul shot).
The story or truth.

definitions.definition: Unfair; shameful.

definitions.definition: An instance of hijacking; the illegal seizure of a vehicle; a hijacking.
An instance of a seizure and redirection of a process.
An amendment which deletes the contents of a bill and inserts entirely new provisions.
Preflop, the position two before the dealer.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
To seize control of some process or resource to achieve a purpose other than its originally intended one.
To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).
To introduce an amendment deleting the contents of a bill and inserting entirely new provisions.
A three-dimensional curve — a spiral wound around a helix.
Furtive, stealthy or catlike.
Thin; lank; lean.
Of a garment: close-fitting; clingy.
The act of inquiring; a seeking of information by asking questions; interrogation; a question or questioning.
Search for truth, information, or knowledge; examination of facts or principles; research; investigation

definitions.definition: A flat pastry pressed with a grid pattern.

definitions.definition: A potato waffle, a savoury flat potato cake with the same kind of grid pattern.
To smash.
Someone who watches or observes.
Someone who keeps vigil.
A guard.
The act of suddenly disturbing anyone; an assault or attack.
A tumultuous assault or quarrel.
The fighting of two or more persons, in a public place, to the terror of others.

definitions.definition: Terror.
To startle from quiet; to alarm.
To frighten; to scare; to frighten away.
Of or relating to an alphabet, especially the characters A to Z, both uppercase and lowercase.
(chiefly British) An area of land; a district, region.

A set region of land having particular human occupation or agreed limits, especially inhabited by members of the same race, speakers of the same language etc., or associated with a given person, occupation, species etc.
The territory of a nation, especially an independent nation state or formerly independent nation; a political entity asserting ultimate authority over a geographical area; a sovereign state.
(usually preceded by “the”) A rural area, as opposed to a town or city; the countryside.
The rock through which a vein runs.
The female genitalia, especially the vagina.
From or in the countryside or connected with it.
Of or connected to country music.
A style of popular music that originated in the folk music of the rural population of the southern and western United States and characterized by string-band instruments and simple melodies.

A facility that melts metals in special furnaces and pours the molten metal into molds to make products. Foundries are usually specified according to the type of metal dealt with: iron foundry, brass foundry, etc.
The act, process, or art of casting metals; founding.
A semiconductor fabrication plant in the microelectronics industry.
A person who is zealously enthusiastic for some cause, especially in religion.
Fanatical.
Showing evidence of possession by a god or demon; frenzied, overzealous.
The support about which a lever pivots.

definitions.definition: A crux or pivot; a central point.
An evergreen tree of the genus Citrus such as Citrus sinensis.
The fruit of an orange tree; a citrus fruit with a slightly sour flavour.
The colour of a ripe fruit of an orange tree, midway between red and yellow.
Orange juice.
Orange coloured and flavoured cordial.
Orange coloured and flavoured soft drink.
To color orange.
To become orange.
The act of deforming, or state of being deformed.
A transformation; change of shape.
The act of entering.
Permission to enter.

definitions.definition: A doorway that provides a means of entering a building.
The act of taking possession.
The start of an insurance contract.
A passageway between terraced houses that provides a means of entering a back garden or yard.
A small room immediately inside the front door of a house or other building, often having an access to a stairway and leading on to other rooms
A small group formed within a church, especially Episcopal, for simple dinner and fellowship, and to help facilitate new friendships
An item in a list, such as an article in a dictionary or encyclopedia.
A record made in a log, diary or anything similarly organized; a datum in a database.

definitions.definition: A term at any position in a matrix.

definitions.definition: The exhibition or depositing of a ship's papers at the customhouse, to procure licence to land goods; or the giving an account of a ship's cargo to the officer of the customs, and obtaining his permission to land the goods.
The point when a musician starts to play or sing; entrance.
An abstract archetype of a given thing, compared to which real-life examples are seen as imperfect approximations; pure essence, as opposed to actual examples.
The conception of someone or something as representing a perfect example; an ideal.
The form or shape of something; a quintessential aspect or characteristic.
An image of an object that is formed in the mind or recalled by the memory.

definitions.definition: More generally, any result of mental activity; a thought, a notion; a way of thinking.
A conception in the mind of something to be done; a plan for doing something, an intention.

definitions.definition: A purposeful aim or goal; intent
definitions.example: Yeah, that's the idea.

definitions.definition: A vague or fanciful notion; a feeling or hunch; an impression.
definitions.example: He had the wild idea that if he leant forward a little, he might be able to touch the mountain-top.

definitions.definition: A musical theme or melodic subject.
Absence of justice; unjustice.
Violation of the rights of another person or people.

definitions.definition: Unfairness; the state of not being fair or just.
A strip of cloth worn around the neck and tied in the front. See also bowtie.
The sense or ability of sight.
Something seen; an object perceived visually.
Something imaginary one thinks one sees.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Something unreal or imaginary; a creation of fancy.
An ideal or a goal toward which one aspires.

definitions.definition: A religious or mystical experience of a supernatural appearance.

definitions.definition: A person or thing of extraordinary beauty.
Pre-recorded film or tape; footage.
To imagine something as if it were to be true.
To present as in a vision.
To provide with a vision.
The knowledge and skill to be able to (do something correctly).

definitions.definition: An advocate of globalism.
definitions.example: You are either a globalist or a nationalist; one cannot be both.

definitions.definition: One who believes that Adolf Hitler intended to extend the Third Reich beyond the continent of Europe.
Of or pertaining to globalism.
(often used in combination with an adjective) Having a certain type of bust (breasts; cleavage).
An often childish display or fit of bad temper.

Things which have had a deposit placed on them and which the owner (generally a store) has agreed to hold for the customer and accept full payment at a later time.
The system of accepting a deposit and holding for a customer to complete purchase.
To place items on hold with a deposit at a merchant's.
A receptacle in a church for holy water, especially one used in baptism.
A receptacle for oil in a lamp.
Spring, source, fountain.

A set of glyphs of unified design, belonging to one typeface (e.g., Helvetica), style (e.g., italic), and weight (e.g., bold). Usually representing the letters of an alphabet and its supplementary characters.
A computer file containing the code used to draw and compose the glyphs of one or more typographic fonts on a computer display or printer.
To overlay (text) on the picture.
A source, wellspring, fount.
To exchange.

definitions.definition: To change (something) to the specified state using a switch.

To change places, tasks, etc.

definitions.definition: To get angry suddenly; to quickly or unreasonably become enraged.
To swing or whisk.

definitions.definition: To trim.

Without effort.
Someone who presents a television programme of videos; a video jockey.
Vagina, vulva.
A cylindrical mechanical fastener that attaches multiple parts together by fitting through a hole and deforming the head(s) at either end.
Any fixed point or certain basis.
A light kind of footman's armour.
To attach or fasten parts by using rivets.
To install rivets.
To command the attention of.
To make firm or immovable.

definitions.definition: An associate in the commission of a crime; a participator in an offense, whether a principal or an accessory.
A cooperator.
Any of various large food and game fishes of the subfamily Epinephelinae, especially the genera Epinephelus and Mycteroperca, which inhabit warm seas.
Something brought in from an exterior source, especially for sale or trade.
The practice of importing.
Significance, importance.

definitions.definition: To bring (something) in from a foreign country, especially for sale or trade.
To load a file into a software application from another version or system.

definitions.definition: One who enters into, or offers for, any service of his/her own free will, especially when done without pay.

One who enters into military service voluntarily, but who, when in service, is subject to discipline and regulations like other soldiers; -- opposed to conscript; specifically, a voluntary member of the organized militia of a country as distinguished from the standing army.
A person who acts out of his own will without a legal obligation, such as a donor.
A plant that grows spontaneously, without being cultivated on purpose; see volunteer plant in Wikipedia.
A native or resident of the American state of Tennessee.
To enlist oneself as a volunteer.
To do or offer to do something voluntarily.

definitions.definition: To offer, usually unprompted.

definitions.definition: To grow without human sowing or intentional cultivation.

Institution of higher education (typically accepting students from the age of about 17 or 18, depending on country, but in some exceptional cases able to take younger students) where subjects are studied and researched in depth and degrees are offered.
definitions.example: The only reason why I haven't gone to university is because I can't afford it.

definitions.definition: To a greater degree or extent.

definitions.definition: Used to form the comparative form of adjectives and adverbs.

definitions.definition: In negative constructions: any further, any longer; any more.
Used in addition to an inflected comparative form.

definitions.definition: A greater number or quantity (of something).

definitions.definition: An extra or additional quantity (of something).

A person who puts out fires.
